results.rendered_html:
1. abhi-saṃ-√dhā (3. du. -dhattas; Imper. 2. sg. -dhehi; aor. -adhāt)
to snap at (acc.) for devouring,  RV. x, 87, 3 ;  AV. ;  ŚBr. ;  Kāṭh. ;
to take aim at (dat. or acc.),  MBh.  &c.;
to aim at, have in view (with acc.; rarely dat.),  ib. ;
to acknowledge unanimously, (perf. 3. pl. -dadhuḥ),  RV. i, 101, 6 ;
to overcome, master,  Mn.  &c.;
to win (as by presents),  R. iv, 54, 5 ;
to ally, associate with (instr.)

results.rendered_html:
abhisaṃdhā 3 U.
1 To hold together
2 To acknowledge, recognize; own.
3 To fit or fix a missile, arrow &c. to the bow.
4 To throw or shoot at, aim at; (śūlaṃ) cikṣepa paramakruddho lakṣmaṇāyābhisaṃhitaṃ  Rām. 
5 To aim at, have in view, think of, (with acc.); padāviddhaṃ yāṃtī skhalitamabhisaṃdhāya bahuśaḥ  V. 4. 28  calling to mind, thinking of my fault; ṛṣyamūkamabhisaṃdhāya  Mv. 5  towards, in the direction of;  Mv. 6 ;  Bg. 17. 12 , 25 ; sometimes with dat.; abhisaṃdadhate ye ca viśvāsāyāsya mānavāḥ  Mb. 
6 To deceive, cheat; janaṃ vidvānekaḥ sakalamabhisaṃdhāya  Māl. 1. 14. 
7 To come to an understanding or agreement; phalaṃ tvanabhisaṃdhāya  Ms. 9. 52  (evaṃ yatra niyamo na kṛtaḥ).
8 To win over, make friendship with, ally oneself with; tān sarvānabhisaṃdadhyātsāmādibhirupakramaiḥ  Ms. 7. 159  (vaśīkuryāt).
9 To declare solemnly, agree, promise.
10 To calumniate, traduce.
11 To add.
12 To prefer.
